name: Chocolate Decadence Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F and place a rack in the center.
2. Butter a 10-inch-diameter springform pan with 2-inch-high sides.
3. Melt both chocolates with espresso and butter over medium-low heat in a heavy saucepan, stirring until smooth. Let cool.
4. Pour the mixture into a large bowl and mix in vanilla extract.
5. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolks with dark brown sugar until a ribbon forms when beaters are lifted. Fold this yolk mixture into the chocolate mixture.
6. Add flour to the chocolate mixture and fold gently.
7. In another large bowl, beat egg whites until almost stiff. Gradually fold in 1/4 of the white mixture into the chocolate mixture to lighten it.
8. Gently fold in the remaining whites.
9. Pour batter into the prepared pan and bake for about 45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out with moist crumbs still attached.
10. Remove from oven and let cool completely on a rack (the cake will fall). If preparing ahead, cover the cake in the pan with plastic wrap and let it stand at room temperature.
11. Run a knife around the sides of the pan to loosen if needed, then release the cake sides.
12. Invert the cake onto a plate and place a stencil or doily on top. Sift powdered sugar over the top.
13. Serve.
